rationale: The citing paper is explicitly built on processing-fluency theory and investigates when fluency effects reverse by content/consumer conditions, while the cited JMKR paper is a core fluency-boundary-condition study showing that metacognitive difficulty can increase evaluations via lay-theory interpretations. Given this strong theoretical/mechanism overlap (not just topical similarity) and a verified DOI match, this pair is plausibly substantive rather than an incidental mention.
